page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. It does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place this server consistently in the average range for most operations. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1815.0 and a multi-core score of 6984.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 13039.39. Memory bandwidth tests show 542.3 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 61.99 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Text processing is a relative strength, scoring 2350.0 in Geekbench, which falls into the top 25% tier. However, LLM text generation on a medium 7B model is a notable bottleneck, yielding a poor 6.1 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the n4-highmem-8 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is appropriate for database operations, caching layers like Redis (which benchmarked at 9.89 million operations per second), software compilation, and general-purpose memory-intensive applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of RAM. It operates with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and a 260 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate average overall performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 13039.39 and a strong Geekbench text processing score of 2350.0. However, it exhibits poor performance in 7B LLM text generation at 6.1 tokens/sec. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. It is best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 8 Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 260 MB L3 cache, delivering average benchmark performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized configuration featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and large L3 cache for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Large 260 MB L3 cache supporting memory-bound operations
Average benchmark performance with 9.89 million Redis SET operations per second
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for database operations, caching, and software compilation
